verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70/HSP72) is predominantly a cytosolic chaperone with no classical TM domain or GPI anchor. However, extensive literature documents tumor-selective and stress-induced plasma membrane surface expression: (1) membrane-bound HSPA1A is detected on a wide variety of human and mouse tumor cell types by flow cytometry using conformational anti-HSP70 mAbs (cmHsp70.1) that recognize an extracellularly exposed epitope; (2) surface localization is mechanistically linked to interaction with sphingoglycolipid Gb3 and/or phosphatidylserine in tumor/stressed cells; (3) the surface pool is demonstrably stress- and cell-state-induced—absent or minimal on matched normal tissues. This qualifies as cell_state_induced / dual_localization contextual surface accessibility. Soluble/secreted and exosomal forms also exist but the documented plasma-membrane-integrated pool in tumor/stressed cells constitutes genuine outer-leaflet accessibility for surface-directed binders.
